Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Doug Atkinson, Esq. (415.793.7819; doug@accidentlawsf.com) of Atkinson & Shenfield Law Firm for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant.
Jill K. Cohoe, Esq. (415.743.2841; jcohoe@steyerlaw.com) of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP for Defendant/Cross-Complainant via Zoom Videoconference.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTS KODORSKI DESIGN, INC., KHODOR BAALBAKI, JOHN GOLDMAN and GOLDMAN ARCHITECTS' Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint.

Cross-defendants' demurrer and motion to strike are both denied with prejudice as untimely.

Under CCP 430.40 and 435, demurrers and motions to strike must be filed within 30 days of a cross-complaint's service unless extended by stipulation or court order. Here, that 30-day period was exceeded. A stipulation extended the time for service and filing, but only for an answer to the cross-complaint. (Andreoli Dec. Ex. 2.)

One order signed for demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Under CCP 430.40 and 435, demurrers and motions to strike must be filed within 30 days of a cross-complaint's service unless extended by stipulation or court order. Here, that 30-day period was exceeded. A stipulation extended the time for service and filing, but only for an answer to the cross-complaint. (Andreoli Dec. Ex. 2.)
